articles.title: French Inflation Unexpectedly Falls Back to ECB’s 2% Target

articles.title: Yen Sinks to Lowest Level Since 1986
articles.summary:
The yen just hit its weakest level against the dollar in four decades. Japan has raised interest rates and spent billions defending its currency. Yet the yen keeps sliding. Mia Glass explains why.

articles.title: Ethiopia Bondholders Criticise IMF for ‘Poorly’ Handled Debt Rework
articles.summary: Ethiopia’s creditors took a swipe at the International Monetary Fund’s handling of the nation’s debt-restructuring process, describing the past two years of discussions as “poorly” managed.

articles.title: Rand Trading Surges as War Spawns Volatility, Central Bank Says
articles.summary: Trading in the rand rose sharply in the first quarter as volatility induced by the Iran war pushed up average daily turnover by as much as 20%, according to the South African Reserve Bank.

articles.title: Shell Sees LNG Trade Flat in 2026 as Hormuz Chokes Supply
articles.summary: Shell Plc expects global liquefied natural gas flows to stagnate this year due to disruptions from the Middle East conflict, before supply growth rebounds in 2027.

articles.title: Japan Nears Longest Postwar Growth Even as Households Struggle
articles.summary: Japan is close to notching its longest economic growth streak since WWII, as the nation weathers higher oil prices from the war in Iran and the central bank’s ongoing quest to normalize interest rates
